details.text: Derivative: الْحَاقَّةُ comes from the word حَقٌّ (Haqq) which means truth but also means deserved. حَقَّ (Haqqa) the verb can mean to materialise, to come to pass or become a reality. حَقِيقَة (Haqeeqah) refers to a reality itself. حَقَّ (Haqqa) can also mean deserved and rightful like in His statement: حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ الْعَذَابُ “The punishment was deserved upon him/them or rightfully and justifiably happened to them”. حَاقٌّ (Haaqq) is the اِسْم الفَاعِل (Ism ul-Fa’il – Noun of the doer) form which in origin is حاقِقٌ (Haaqiq). حاقَّة (Haaqqah) comes from that form as the feminine form of it. The Taa’ Marbootah (ة) at the end of الْحَاقَّةُ is either to indicate that it is a one of occurrence or for the purpose of hyperbole and emphasis. Its meaning can be understood to mean: The ultimate reality that is bound to happen. Or the matter that is rightfully going to come to pass. The اِسْم الفَاعِل (Ism ul-Fa’il – Noun of the doer) also implies a greater level of immediacy than the present tense verb form المُضارِع i.e., الْحَاقَّةُ implies the immediacy of the impending event.

الْحَاقَّةُ

- The statement of الْحَاقَّةُ (Al-Haaqqah) consists of a Mubtada’ (subject) without a Khabar (predicate or information about the subject), thus making it an incomplete sentence. This is done as a rhetorical style in Arabic which is done to catch the attention of the listener.

Derivative: رَابِيَةً from رَبَا – يَرْبُو – رِبَاء which means to grow and increase. Here it describes the punishment continues to increase in its severity or its grip continues to tighten more and more.
Derivative: تَعِيَ and وَاعِيَةٌ come from وَعَى – يَعِي – وَعْي which means to pay attention and have a strong awareness.

Derivative: دُكَّتَا and دَكَّةً are from دَكَّ which means to demolish or smash something until it is smooth. The image is of the mountains and ground being smashed into each other and flattened to powder.
Derivative: الْوَاقِعَةُ means that which was bound to fall i.e., the great event of the final hour and Day of Judgement (will happen and come to pass).
Derivative: انْشَقَّتِ from اِنْشَقَّ – يَنْشَقُّ – اِنْشِقَاق means to split or tear apart.
Derivative: وَاهِيَةٌ means weak, frail and infirm i.e., that which held the sky up will become too weak to maintain it leading to its collapse.
Derivative: أَرْجَاء refers to the edges or periphery or perimeter areas of a place.
Derivative: هَاؤُمُ is an expression to show jubilation in a result or accomplishment meaning: “Come here and take a look at this”.
Derivative: عِيشَةٍ means a life which you live and implies a life that is full, good and providing.
Derivative: رَاضِيَةٍ mans “pleasing”.
Derivative: هَنِيئًا means to be in comfort and ease without any concerns or worries.
Derivative: أَسْلَفْتُمْ from أَسْلَفَ which means to have done something in the past that was good.
Derivative: الْخَالِيَةِ in origin it means empty, devoid or free (of something). When attached to time it means: “Bygone” or “past” i.e., the days that have passed by.
Derivative: الْقَاضِيَةَ – Means to be terminated and to be finished. Here it is in reference to the death of the first life i.e., if only it had been “final” and there was no resurrection.

Derivative: هَلَكَ means to destroy. The meaning here is “My authority and standing (i.e., which I enjoyed in the first life) has been taken away from me” however instead of using the usual verb for taking away, Allah uses هَلَكَ emphasising that it has been ripped away from him, become utterly destroyed to nothing and is unrecoverable.
Derivative: أَدْرَا is أَدْرَى and it meaning is like أعْرَفَ – يُعْرِفُ which means to inform or let someone know about something, or to give you an idea or some indication about it.
- “What will make you know (or give you an idea/clue) about what something is or about something’s reality”.
Derivative: غُلُّو is the command form of غَلَّ which means to place an iron collar or shackles on someone, or to constrain someone.

Derivative: غِسْلِينٍ can refer to that which is left over after washing something in terms of filth. It has also been said to refer to the pus of the people of hellfire and also that it refers to the tree in hellfire. It is agreed that it refers to that which is vile, disgusting and unclean or unwholesome.

- فَلَا here before an oath أُقْسِمُ (I swear) does not mean a negation of the following statement. Rather, the لَا (No) is separated from أُقْسِمُ (I swear). لَا (No) here means: “No or Nay (to all that you say or believe)”.
Derivative: الْقَارِعَةِ refers to the great knocking or striking calamity or destruction. It comes from قَرَعَ (Qara’a) which means to knock, strike or thump, often used for banging on a door.

Derivative: قَوْلُ (Qawl) is from قَالَ – يَقولُ – قَوْلًا. كَلام (Kalaam) refers to one’s own speech. قَوْلُ (Qawl) is what you say or the statement you make, but the origin of the speech may not be your own.
Derivative: الْأَقَاوِيلِ means statements and is in a form of plural (جَمْعُ القِلَّة – Jam’u l-Qillah) that indicates a small number, nine or less.
Derivative: تَقَوَّلَ – تَقَوَّلًا means to make up speech or attribute a statement falsely.
Derivative: الْيَمِينِ means the right hand and it is an expression that means force and power.
Derivative: حَاجِزِينَ means to act as a barrier i.e., to protect him.

Derivative: الطَّاغِيَةِ and comes from طَغَى that means to rebel. The word here has been understood in two ways. Its meaning depends on the understanding of the بِ that proceeds it بِالطَّاغِيَةِ. If it is understood to be باء السَّبَبِيَة (Baa’ As-Sababiyah – Baa’ of causation), then it means (they were destroyed) “because of الطَّاغِيَةِ” in which case it would mean excess and rebellion. If the Baa’ however carries the meaning of “by” it could mean that they were destroyed by الطَّاغِيَةِ in which case it would refer to the name of punishment that they were destroyed with and this punishment is names الطَّاغِيَةِ (At-Taghiyah) because it was a punishment that went against and rebelled against the normal subdued nature that Allah made for us, likes the winds and seas.
- حَقُّ الْيَقِينِ – “The certain truth”. It means the conviction that is based upon experience and what has been ascertained.

Derivative: صَرْصَرٍ means extremely bitterly cold that burns you due to its severity and is a description for wind. The repetition in صَرْصَرٍindicates that this wind and its bitter cold hits you over and over again i.e., it is unrelenting.
Derivative: عَاتِيَةٍ means violent and penetrating.
Derivative: سَخَّرَ – تَسْخِير means to make something do what you want it to do. To tame something by command and to subdue and subjugate.
Derivative: حُسُومًا means that which erases all traces of anything.
Derivative: صَرْعَىٰ refers to what has been flung to the ground and is sprawled across it in reference to the dead bodies that have perished in the punishment.
Derivative: الْمُؤْتَفِكَاتُ from اِئتفَكَ which means to be turned upside down or turned upon their heads. الْمُؤْتَفِكَاتُ refers to the nations that were turned upside down.
